description: Trigger discrimination for the ROLLOUT strategy of a change (this skill: temporary de-risking) vs the permanent ENTITLEMENT model (plan-entitlement-architect), vs role PERMISSIONS (authorization-matrix-designer), vs the EXPERIMENT design a flag may host (ab-test-designer).

cases.prompt: Roll out this risky change safely: staged from internal to a percentage to GA, guardrail metrics that auto-roll-back, a kill switch, and a plan to remove the flag afterward.
cases.note: SEAM CASE (primary) — staged delivery, guardrails, kill switch, and flag removal are a temporary rollout strategy. Nothing about permanent plan/role gating appears.
cases.reason: De-risking a change into production via a temporary release flag is this skill's core scope, distinct from any permanent entitlement or permission.

cases.prompt: Decide which features and usage limits each pricing plan includes and how that's enforced consistently across UI, API, and jobs.
cases.note: SEAM CASE (primary, reverse — hard seam) — plan × feature × limit is a permanent entitlement model, not a temporary rollout. A flag here would drift from the billing source of truth.
cases.reason: plan-entitlement-architect owns durable plan gating. A release flag is meant to be removed; an entitlement is meant to persist. Different lifetime, different owner.

cases.prompt: Define which roles can perform which actions on which resources — the roles × permissions matrix with deny-by-default.
cases.note: SEAM CASE (reverse, hard seam) — who MAY do what by role is authorization, a permanent security model, not a rollout gate.
cases.reason: authorization-matrix-designer owns role/permission design. A rollout flag controls exposure of a change over time, not standing authority.

cases.prompt: Design the experiment behind this flag: hypothesis, primary and guardrail metrics, sample size and duration, and how we read significance without peeking.
cases.note: SEAM CASE (cross-pack, D12.6) — hypothesis, sample size, and significance readout are experiment design. The flag may host the experiment, but the statistics belong to ab-test-designer.
cases.reason: ab-test-designer owns experiment design and analysis. This skill carries the rollout mechanics of the flag, not the experiment's validity.
